transcript: Your sneakers. Pristine. White. Clean. Beautiful. Well, they used to be, but now they're covered in dirt and stains. I'm afraid it's too late for them. You have to throw them away. Just kidding. Thomas with Real Men Real Style here. And today, I'm going to show you how to easily bring your white sneakers back to life. don't worry in this video we're gonna go over both canvas and leather sneakers step number one throw them in the washing machine although you can clean your canvas sneakers in the washing machine this puts a lot of excess wear and tear on them and throwing your leather sneakers in the washing machine is a hard no this will leave a lot of creases in the upper and will dry out the leather let's try again step number one preparation cover your workspace with newspaper or a rag now remove the laces from your shoes we're going to clean these separately I recommend putting in shoe trees these will help your sneakers keep their shape during the cleaning process and make it much easier to scrub away at the stains step number two remove the dirt before we get to the heavy-duty cleaning you want to take a soft bristle brush preferably made from horsehair and wipe away any loose dirt failure to do this means you'll be rubbing all the dirt and grit into your shoes later while you're trying to clean which can easily scratch your leather sneakers work the brush over entire shoe, making sure to get into all the gaps and crevices. Step number three, shoe cleaner. Many brands sell cleaners specifically designed to work with your sneakers. This particular bottle comes with a built-in applicator sponge. You could also use a regular sponge or a sneaker brush. Take a wet cloth and use it to dampen your shoes. Now, take the cleaner and gently scrub it over the entire sneaker. Any spots with tougher stains may require extra scrubbing to work it out. When you're finished, wipe off the excess cleaner and let the shoes dry for 30 minutes. This method is effective, but what if I told you you can get the same results with things you already have at home? Step number four, toothpaste. That's right, gents, only does toothpaste clean your teeth, it does wonders for your sneakers too. Take an old worn out toothbrush, or if you like to live life on the wild side, your regular toothbrush, and a tube of toothpaste. Stick with a plain white toothpaste. Avoid gels or paste with color. You don't want to risk creating new stains while trying to get rid of the old ones. Apply a generous glob of toothpaste to the brush and start scrubbing in small circular motions. The mild abrasives in the toothpaste will help lift and scrub away the stains. Work over the entire surface of the shoe, applying additional toothpaste as needed. Again, give extra attention to any tough stains or discoloration. Once you're done, let the sneaker sit for 10 minutes, then wipe off old remaining all remaining toothpaste if any stains remain repeat the process again pro tip with any product to use make sure to spot test it first apply a little bit to the tongue where it'll be hidden so you can make sure it won't discolor or damage your shoe the toothpaste method works amazingly on leather sneakers but it may not cut it with tougher stains on canvas so what's's your other option? Step number five, detergent. Sometimes you'll need something a bit stronger to break through those tough stains. Add a tablespoon of laundry detergent to a bowl and fill it up with warm water. Shoot for 100 degrees Fahrenheit or 40 degrees Celsius. Now take a brush with medium stiff bristles. You'll want something a bit stronger to work on these stains dip into the detergent and begin scrubbing the shoe work in small circular motions making sure to get into all the gaps and crevices rinse your shoes in the sink until you've removed all the detergent let the shoe set out overnight until dry quick tip if you're having trouble getting all the marks off the sneaker soles use a mr clean magic eraser a great job. Step number seven, clean the laces. Now let's tackle those dirty shoelaces. We're going to go and take those laces and put them in the bowl with the laundry detergent and let them soak for 20 minutes. Grab that old toothbrush again and scrub the laces until clean. Give them a quick rinse in the sink and leave them out to dry. Step number six, conditioning. The cleaning process will strip away valuable moisture from your leather shoes which can lead to cracks forming over time it's important to use a lot of the shoe conditioner to restore the lost moisture and keep the leather soft and supple apply the conditioner to a clean rag then work it over the entire upper of the shoe wipe off any excess conditioner and let the shoes sit to dry for 30 minutes. Step number eight, protection. There's no sense in cleaning your white shoes just to get them dirty all over again. Keep them fresh longer by applying a protective shoe spray. Read the can to make sure it's compatible with the material of your sneakers before applying. Apply two light coats of the spray a few minutes apart, then let it sit to dry for an hour. Once dry, re-lace you're good to go there you have it gents your sneakers are looking fresh and new again but what about your favorite pair of boots that are worn out are they a lost cause or can they also be brought back to life not to worry gents I show you how to easily restore your boots in this video right here.
